name: Cherry Crumb Bars
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup cold unsalted butter, cut into 8 pieces
2 1/4 cups canned sour cherries, drained (reserve 1 cup)
3 tbsp cold unsalted butter, cut into 4 pieces
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Line an 8-inch square baking pan with foil, allowing ends to extend above the pan on two sides.
Prepare shortbread: Pulse flour, sugar, and salt in a food processor to combine. Scatter butter over top and pulse just until a dough forms. Press dough gently over the bottom of the prepared pan. Pour egg white over dough and tilt the pan, spreading it evenly. Remove excess egg white with a spatula. Bake until golden brown, 20-25 minutes.
Prepare filling and topping: In a medium saucepan, bring 3/4 cup cherry juice, sugar, and salt to a boil. Whisk in cornstarch into the remaining cherry juice (cold), then whisk into the boiling mixture until thickened, boiled, and clear, about 3-4 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in cherries.
Prepare topping: Pulse flour, both sugars, and salt briefly to combine. Add oats and butter, pulsing just until evenly blended. Reduce oven temperature to 425°F. Spread filling evenly over the base, then sprinkle topping evenly over the filling. Bake until topping is lightly browned, about 25 minutes, then broil until the topping is a slightly deeper brown, watching carefully that it doesn't burn. Allow to cool completely in the pan on a wire rack before lifting foil by the ends onto a cutting board. Cut into 16 squares; remove from foil.
